rules.summary: Unless otherwise stated:
In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:
[deleted.]
A firm must identify its important business services.
A firm must set an impact tolerance for each of its important business services.
The impact tolerance set for each important business service must specify the first point at which a disruption to the important business service would pose a risk to:
The impact tolerance set for each important business service must specify the length of or point in time, in addition to any other relevant metrics, for which a disruption to that _important busin
A firm must ensure it can remain within its impact tolerance for each important business service in the event of a severe but plausible disruption to its operations.
Where a firm is a member of a group, the firm must ensure it accounts for any additional risks arising elsewhere within its group that may affect the firm’s ability to comply with [2.5](/pra
A firm must comply with 2.5 within a reasonable time of the rule coming into effect and in any event by no later than 31 M
A firm must have in place sound, effective and comprehensive strategies, processes and systems that enable it adequately to:
The strategies, processes and systems required by 3.1 must be proportionate to the nature, scale and complexity of the _firm
As part of its obligation under 3.1, a firm must identify and document the necessary people, processes, technology, facili
As part of its obligation under 3.1, a firm must carry out regular scenario testing of its ability to remain within its _i
In carrying out the scenario testing required by 5.1, a firm must identify an appropriate range of adverse circumstances o
The scenario testing required by 5.1 must be proportionate to the nature, scale and complexity of the firm's activities.
A firm must prepare and regularly update a written self-assessment of its compliance with this Part.
The content and level of detail of a firm’s written self-assessment must be proportionate to the nature, scale and complexity of the firm’s activities, and where applicable to the activities of th
A firm must maintain, and be able to provide to the PRA on request, a current version of its written self-assessment, together with all versions produced during the preceding three years.
A firm must ensure that its management body approves the important business services identified by the firm in compliance with [2.1](/pra-rules/operational-resilience#e73f46dfdb044408a10c7fe4d
A firm must ensure that its management body approves the impact tolerances set by the firm in compliance with 2.2.
A firm must ensure that its management body approves and regularly reviews the self-assessment required by 6.1.
[deleted.]
[deleted.]
[deleted.]
[deleted.]
[deleted.]
A CRR consolidation entity must identify each important group business service.
A CRR consolidation entity must set an impact tolerance for each important group business service.
A CRR consolidation entity must assess whether each member of the CRR consolidation entity’s consolidation group providing each important group business service could remain within the _impact
The impact tolerance set for each important group business service must specify the first point at which a disruption to the important group business service would pose a risk to:
The impact tolerance set for each important group business service must specify the length of or point in time, in addition to any other relevant metrics, for which a disruption to that _important
A CRR consolidation entity must have in place sound, effective and comprehensive strategies, processes and systems that enable it adequately to:
A CRR consolidation entity must ensure that its management body approves:
The strategies, processes and systems required by this Chapter must be proportionate to the nature, scale and complexity
A CRR consolidation entity must comply with 8.6 to [8.13](/pra-rules/operational-resilience#8442bc3b8b124fecbad88d1d1e7568
